data: Group Discussion - Overqualification — Boon or Bane
Group Discussion - Overqualification — Boon or Bane
Learn how to discuss overqualification in a group discussion

message:
Hey! Today we're learning how to discuss Overqualification — Boon or Bane in a group discussion.

Using the right structure helps you speak clearly and confidently.
image_url: https://r2-activity-data.gosupernova.live/system-uploads/scenario-data/ac97b412-5884-4fb6-82c6-42597079df71-GD---v2-(1).jpg
button_text: Cool!

message:
When you want to talk about what something offers or contributes, use this structure:

"[Topic] offers [advantage]."

For example: "Overqualification offers a higher level of expertise to the organization."
image_url: https://r2-activity-data.gosupernova.live/system-uploads/scenario-data/ac97b412-5884-4fb6-82c6-42597079df71-GD---v2-(1).jpg
button_text: Got it!

message:
Here's another example.

If someone asks about the benefits of overqualification, you can say:

"Overqualification offers better problem-solving ability to the team."
image_url: https://r2-activity-data.gosupernova.live/system-uploads/scenario-data/ac97b412-5884-4fb6-82c6-42597079df71-GD---v2-(1).jpg
button_text: Next!

message:
When you want to mention both the advantage and the drawback, use this structure:

"While [topic] offers [advantage], it also has a [drawback]."

For example: "While overqualification offers advanced skills, it also has a risk of higher salary demands."
image_url: https://r2-activity-data.gosupernova.live/system-uploads/scenario-data/ac97b412-5884-4fb6-82c6-42597079df71-GD---v2-(1).jpg
button_text: Makes sense!

message:
Let's practice discussing overqualification!

image_url:
button_text: Start Practice!

Select the correct way to talk about what overqualification offers

1.Overqualification offers a higher level of expertise to the organization.
2.Overqualification is always a problem for any company.

Correct answer: 1

Select the correct way to talk about overqualification's drawback

1.Overqualification is too risky and companies should never hire overqualified candidates.
2.While overqualification offers expertise, it also has the challenge of employee dissatisfaction.

Correct answer: 2

first_message: That was incredible! Now we're going to practice by answering few questions in English. Can we start?
prompt_id: 4d3f5213-fcc4-40f3-879d-051908377a31
questions: "'1. What does overqualification offer to a company? Use Structure 1.'

'2. While overqualification offers experience, what challenge does it also bring? Respond using Structure 2.'

'3. What does overqualification offer to the hiring process? Use Structure 1.'

'4. While overqualification offers skills, what challenge does it also bring? Give your answer using Structure 2.'

'5. What does overqualification offer to the organization's growth? Use Structure 1.'"
